name: Routes list: filter by starred and tag (API + UI)
description: Layer annotation-aware filtering on top of the route-list-filters feature. Users should be able to jump to "only my starred routes" or "only routes tagged road-trip" from the same filter bar they already use for date/duration/etc. This is a small delta on both ends (a couple of query params, a star checkbox, and a tag selector) so it is bundled into a single feature that touches backend and frontend.
acceptance_criteria: GET /v1/route/:dongle_id accepts starred=true|false and tag=<name> (repeatable: ?tag=a&tag=b means has all of these tags, documented in the handler); tag values are normalized (lowercased+trimmed) before matching.

The underlying sqlc query is extended so: starred=true filters to routes.starred=true, starred=false is equivalent to routes.starred=false, and each tag= adds an EXISTS subquery against route_tags (so multi-tag is an AND, not an OR).
Handler tests cover: starred=true, starred=false, single tag, multi-tag AND, unknown tag returns empty set (no error), combined with existing filters (e.g. starred=true&from=...).

The filter bar in web/src/app/routes/page.tsx gains a Starred-only checkbox and a Tags multi-select (chip picker populated from GET /v1/devices/:dongle_id/tags); selecting tags narrows the list, starred toggles route.starred.
URL querystring sync includes the new params; Clear filters wipes them along with the rest.
go test ./internal/api/..., go vet ./..., pnpm --dir web lint, pnpm --dir web type-check, and pnpm --dir web test pass.
notes:
Backend: extended GET /v1/route/:dongle_id with starred=true|false and repeatable tag=<name>. Added starred narg to the sqlc CountRoutesByDeviceFiltered query; added a new hand-written CountRoutesByDeviceFilteredCustom + updated ListRoutesByDeviceFiltered in routes_custom.go to dynamically append EXISTS-against-route_tags subqueries (one per tag, AND semantics). Chose dynamic SQL over a static sqlc query because the number of EXISTS clauses depends on user input; keeping tag AND in the custom wrapper preserves the existing single-query-per-request contract rather than forcing a post-filter in Go. Tags are lowercased+trimmed in the handler via normalizeTagFilter before hitting the db layer so they match the normalization SetRouteTags already applies on writes. Unknown tags return an empty set (no error). Handler tests added: starred=true/false, invalid starred, single tag, multi-tag AND, unknown tag -> empty, starred combined with from= date range, tag normalization (lowercase + trim whitespace), duplicate-tag dedupe, zero-tag emits no EXISTS subqueries. Also added tests asserting starred/tag args match between count and list, and that SQL placeholders renumber correctly when tags are present.

Frontend: FilterBar gained a Starred-only checkbox (two-state: off / on) and a Tags multi-select chip picker populated from GET /v1/devices/:dongle_id/tags. Tag picker hides already-selected tags from the dropdown, renders selected tags as removable chips, and falls back to a disabled 'No tags available' option when the device has no tags yet. Starred checkbox intentionally only emits ?starred=true (not ?starred=false) from the UI -- uncommon view, deep link still works via the API. URL sync: ?starred=true round-trips via filtersToSearchParams/filtersFromSearchParams; tags emit as repeated ?tag=a&tag=b entries that the backend picks up via c.QueryParams()['tag']. Tag values are normalized (lowercase + trim) on both sides so pasted URLs reconstruct the exact backend filter. RouteListItem TS type grew note/starred/tags/preserved fields to match the response shape established by route-annotations-api. Clear filters wipes starredOnly and tags alongside the existing filters; EMPTY_FILTERS / isDefaultFilters updated accordingly.

Verified: go test ./..., go vet ./..., pnpm --dir web lint, pnpm --dir web type-check, pnpm --dir web build, ./.projd/scripts/smoke.sh (lint + typecheck + vet + fmt) all pass. pnpm --dir web test is a no-op because web/package.json defines no test script (same as prior waves).
